cues.label: Order matters
cues.headline: 1-2 and 2-1 are different
cues.narration: Permutations use every element once and care about order, so you cannot use a forward-only start index.

cues.label: Used-array
cues.headline: One flag per element
cues.narration: A boolean used-array tracks which elements are already in the path; the loop skips any that are used.

cues.label: Choose
cues.headline: Set the flag, push
cues.narration: To choose an element, set its used flag and append it, then recurse to fill the next position.

cues.label: Record
cues.headline: Full length is an answer
cues.narration: When the path length equals the input length, you have a full permutation, so record a copy.

cues.label: Un-choose
cues.headline: Pop and clear the flag
cues.narration: Un-choose pops the element and clears its flag, freeing it for a different branch.

cues.label: Prune
cues.headline: Skip illegal branches early
cues.narration: With constraints like N-Queens, test the partial state before recursing and skip doomed branches — deleting whole subtrees.
